core-orchestration.title: Multi-agent orchestration system
core-orchestration.description: Orchestrator + Dispatcher + 6 sub-agents with ADR-0001 single-entry-point architecture
done_criteria: Orchestrator handles full conversation lifecycle (greet → triage → dispatch → integrate → close)
Dispatcher returns structured intent classification with confidence scores
All 6 sub-agents follow ADR-0002 communication protocol
Permission tiers (L0/L1/L2) enforced per ADR-0004

mcp-integrations.title: MCP server integrations (order-server + customer-service)
mcp-integrations.description: Two local stdio MCP servers exposing order queries, ticket CRUD, returns, FAQ, and analytics tools
done_criteria: order-server exposes 10 read-only tools for order/customer/shipment queries
customer-service exposes 13 tools across KB, ticket, returns, survey, analytics domains
Both servers launch automatically via .claude/mcp.json

emotional-escalation.title: Three-tier emotional escalation ladder (ADR-0003)
emotional-escalation.description: L1 Orchestrator → L2 complaint-agent → L3 human-handoff-agent with keyword triggers
done_criteria: L2 triggers: keywords 投诉/曝光/律师/监管/经理/315/起诉/法院
L3 triggers: self-harm, violence, legal threats → immediate transfer, zero soothing
L1 de-escalation handled by Orchestrator before routing

customer-verification.title: Customer verification (OTP + scoped tokens)
customer-verification.description: JWT-based OTP verification with customer-scoped and order-scoped tokens per ADR-0005
done_criteria: Customer-scoped token authorizes all orders owned by that customer
Order-scoped token authorizes only that specific order
Unscoped token rejected for protected resources

analytics-telemetry.title: Usage analytics + daily reporting
analytics-telemetry.description: Metadata-only usage events with daily Markdown report generation per ADR-0005
done_criteria: Usage events record conversation metadata (no raw messages)
Daily reports interpret business days in REPORT_TIMEZONE (Asia/Shanghai)
Failed runs recorded before re-raise
data-analysis-agent generates daily reports via MCP tool

rag-knowledge-base.title: RAG FAQ knowledge base
rag-knowledge-base.description: Semantic FAQ retrieval with embedding model fallback to lexical index
done_criteria: search_faq returns ranked results with scores
get_faq_categories lists all categories with counts
get_faq_by_id retrieves single FAQ entry
consultation-agent queries KB for product/tutorial/activity/FAQ answers

satisfaction-survey.title: Satisfaction survey workflow
satisfaction-survey.description: Post-conversation 1-5 star survey with low-score follow-up ticket creation
done_criteria: Survey triggers after 2 rounds of no-new-questions + positive signal
4-5 stars: thank + invite comments
1-3 stars: apologize + create 低分回访 work order + escalate to human-handoff

ticket-lifecycle.title: Ticket lifecycle management
ticket-lifecycle.description: Full ITIL ticket CRUD: new → assigned → in_progress → pending → resolved → closed
done_criteria: create_ticket with type/priority/description
get_ticket with full history and notes
list_tickets with status/assignee filters and pagination
update_ticket for status transitions and reassignment
search_tickets by keyword across title/description/ticket_number

return-workflow.title: Return/exchange/refund workflow
return-workflow.description: After-sales return lifecycle: pending → approved → in_transit → received → refunded → completed
done_criteria: create_return with type (return/exchange/refund) and reason
get_return with full details including RMA number
list_returns with status/customer_id filters
update_return_status advances through lifecycle

production-deployment.title: Production deployment hardening (P1)
production-deployment.description: MySQL migration (sequence adapter + connection pool + prod compose), secrets management (_FILE support + rotation runbook), monitoring (histogram metrics + Prometheus/Grafana/Alertmanager stack), CI/CD (lint + test matrix + migration smoke + GHCR build-push)
done_criteria: Multi-process safe number generation via dialect-aware sequencer (InProcess for SQLite, MysqlCounter for MySQL)
MySQL connection pool configured (pool_recycle=3600, pool_size/max_overflow env-tunable)
Production compose overlay (docker-compose.prod.yml) with APP_ENV=production and Docker secrets
Startup migration via docker-entrypoint.sh (alembic upgrade head before uvicorn)
Secret rotation runbook documented (JWT/OIDC, AUTH_DEV_SECRET, MySQL password)
Request latency histogram in /api/metrics + Prometheus/Grafana/Alertmanager monitoring stack
CI pipeline: ruff lint + Python 3.10/3.11/3.12 test matrix + MySQL 8.4 migration smoke
CD pipeline: build-push to GHCR on main/tag

concurrency-load-testing.title: Multi-agent concurrency and load testing (P2)
concurrency-load-testing.description: Fixed 3 concurrency bugs (OrderedDict race, SQLite no-WAL, idempotency TOCTOU) and established 3-layer concurrency test system (L1 integration / L2 API / L3 load script)
done_criteria: _CONVERSATION_STATES protected by RLock with double-checked locking (DB I/O outside lock)
SQLite WAL mode + busy_timeout=5000 + synchronous=NORMAL via event listener
run_idempotent handles IntegrityError on concurrent insert (rollback + return cached)
18 concurrency tests pass (isolation + numbering + idempotency + WAL)
4 load tests pass (P95 < 2s, QPS > 0, tail latency < 5s, no state leak)
Standalone load script (scripts/loadtest/load_orchestrator.py) with QPS/P50/P95/P99 output

ci-tooling-hardening.title: CI & tooling hardening (P3 Phase A)
ci-tooling-hardening.description: Coverage tracking, dependency audit, full table migration smoke test, readiness probe fix, OpenAPI docs, exception narrowing, load test CI governance
done_criteria: pytest-cov configured with branch coverage, fail_under=60, actual coverage >74%
pip-audit CI job scanning dependencies for known vulnerabilities
migration-smoke verifies all 15 tables from Base.metadata (was 4/15)
/api/ready returns HTTP 503 when degraded (was 200), no inspect() table introspection
OpenAPI docs with title/description/8 tags, production disables docs_url
4 bare-except narrowed: 3 to SQLAlchemyError, 1 to (OSError, ImportError, RuntimeError)
CI test job uses -m 'not load', separate non-blocking load-test job
test_ready_returns_503_when_db_down passes

structured-logging-rate-limiting.title: Structured logging (structlog) + API rate limiting (slowapi) (P4 Phase B)
structured-logging-rate-limiting.description: structlog 26.x with ProcessorFormatter stdlib bridge (JSON prod / colored dev), contextvars request_id binding via pure ASGI middleware, slowapi per-route rate limiting (OTP 5/min, orchestrator 60/min, write 30/min, read 120/min) with env-driven config
done_criteria: structlog configured with ProcessorFormatter bridging stdlib logging
Production outputs JSON, development outputs colored console
Pure ASGI middleware binds request_id/method/path/status_code/duration to contextvars
Existing LOGGER.warning calls in orchestrator_runtime.py automatically include request_id
MCP server logs to stderr (stdout reserved for JSON-RPC protocol)
29 routes have @limiter.limit() decorators with request: Request parameter
Health endpoints excluded from rate limiting
Rate limit values configurable via RATE_LIMIT_* env vars
Tests pass with RATE_LIMIT_ENABLED=false default; rate limit tests in dedicated file

prometheus-client-xdist.title: prometheus_client migration + pytest-xdist parallel tests (P5 Phase C)
prometheus-client-xdist.description: Replaced custom MetricsRegistry with prometheus_client standard types (Histogram + Gauge with multiprocess mode support), /api/metrics outputs standard exposition format; added pytest-xdist with --dist=loadscope for 5x test speedup
done_criteria: prometheus_client replaces custom MetricsRegistry with standard Counter/Gauge/Histogram types
/api/metrics returns generate_latest() with CONTENT_TYPE_LATEST header
Multi-process mode supported via PROMETHEUS_MULTIPROC_DIR env var
record_request(route, start_time) function signature unchanged (request_logging.py unmodified)
pytest-xdist with -n auto --dist=loadscope runs all tests in parallel
Test suite runtime reduced from ~63s to ~13s (5x speedup)
Load tests remain serial (not affected by xdist)
No test files or conftest.py modified for xdist (process-level isolation)
